This pull request fixes a bug introduced by a recent contribution that caused startup failure for the Llama-3.2-11b. The issue occurred because the model architecture MllamaForConditionalGeneration was not properly recognized as an encoder-decoder model. The wrong contribution is as the f

sglang.title: Fix MLA decode path returning unwritten (padded) rows
sglang.author: clintg6
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
This PR fixes a correctness issue in the MLA decode path where we used q_mask from pad_sequence_with_mask() to select rows from the mla_decode_fwd output buffer.
The underlying MLA kernel only writes the first qo_indptr[-1] rows (packed/compacted output), leaving the remaining padded rows un

sglang.title: [AMD] Use bfloat16 for correction_bias in AITER FP8 path to avoid runtime dtype conversion for dsv3
sglang.author: inkcherry
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
In the AITER (AMD MI355X) path with FP8 quantization, e_score_correction_bias was initialized in float32, but at runtime in biased_grouped_topk it gets cast to match gating_output.dtype:
correction_bias.to(dtype=gating_output.dtype)
On the AITER path, aiter_dsv3_router_gemm returns logits

sglang.title: [GDN][Qwen3-Next][Qwen3.5] Fuse fused_gdn_gating and fused_recurrent_gated_delta_rule_update in verify_target
sglang.author: yuan-luo
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

There are three key kernels in GDN. This PR is concerned with the last kernel.

for extend
for decode
for verify_target

Currently in GDN, verify path uses fused_gdn_gating + fused_recurrent_gated_delta_rule_update. Our goal is to fuse them into one kernel, so as to improve performance.

sglang.title: Migrate renorm kernels from sgl-kernel to FlashInfer JIT
sglang.author: Johnsonms
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Migrate top_k_renorm_probs, top_p_renorm_probs, and top_k_mask_logits from compiled sgl-kernel CUDA implementations to FlashInfer's JIT-compiled public API. This reduces the sgl-kernel wheel size and leverages FlashInfer's optimized and maintained implementations.
Changes:

Use flashinfer.sampling p

sglang.title: [Kernel] Dispatch exp/sin/cos through dtype_trait
sglang.author: xingsy97
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
exp/sin/cos in math.cuh are hardcoded with float signatures, while all other math functions (abs, sqrt, rsqrt, max, min) dispatch through dtype_trait<T>. This inconsistency allows silent implicit conversion when half/bfloat16 values are passed.
Addresses review feedback from #19770.
Modif

sglang.title: [Core] Unify `max_num_reqs` `dp_size` division for pool sizing
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
When --enable-dp-attention is on, max_running_requests is a global value (across all dp workers). Each worker's pool should be sized with max_running_requests // dp_size.
Before: only mamba + speculative decoding did // dp_size (added in #16138 as a targeted fix for mamba intermediate state

sglang.title: Fix Kimi K2.5 PP layer range exposure for PD disaggregation
sglang.author: yafengio
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Kimi K2.5 shows repeated outputs in PP>1 + PD. Root cause: the wrapper does not expose start_layer/end_layer, so each PP rank initializes KV cache with start_layer=0.
Add start_layer/end_layer passthrough in KimiK25ForConditionalGeneration to restore correct PP KV sharding.

Modification

Support AITER MLA for num_heads < 16 (e.g., TP=8 with Kimi K2.5 giving 8 heads/rank, or Kimi-Linear giving 4 heads/rank). Uses head-repeat to expand to 16 heads before calling the AITER MLA decode kernel, then contracts the output back. This reuses the existing optimized gqa_ratio=16 ASM ke

This breaks prefix cache after elastic ep reconfigure, since it

self.sliding_window[0] != -1 to the same condition, which causes the
unified_attention path to

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Making entrypoints more deterministic on ROCm
vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Adding some options that are known to render tests' results more deterministic on ROCm. This particular PR targets pytest -s -v entrypoints/openai/test_realtime_validation.py. This test was tested in a loop 10 times before pushing the fix (all of them passed).
cc @kenroche

vllm.title: feat: expose media_io_kwargs at runtime
vllm.author: milesial
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Exposes a new top-level field media_io_kwargs in the OAI chat API, allowing runtime control of media decoding parameters (in addition of init-time --media-io-kwargs). Similar to the existing mm_processor_kwargs.
When no such field is present in the request, the default defined at init-time w
